formulas.name: Trilateral Commission
formulas.notes: A private organization of influential individuals from the US, Europe, and Asia that promotes US interests in global governance.

formulas.name: Pentagon's Military Alliance System
formulas.notes: A network of military alliances led by the US that aims to contain the spread of communism and ensure US interests in various regions.

formulas.name: Trilateral Commission's Three Pillars
formulas.notes: The three pillars of the Trilateral Commission's agenda, which include: 1) Economic interdependence, 2) Security cooperation, and 3) Democratic values.

formulas.name: Bush Doctrine
formulas.notes: A foreign policy doctrine introduced by President George W. Bush in 2002, which includes preventative war and regime change as tools for maintaining US security and promoting democracy.

formulas.name: Monroe Doctrine
formulas.notes: A US foreign policy doctrine introduced in 1823, which asserts that the US would not tolerate European intervention in the Americas and would defend the region's independence.

formulas.name: Bretton Woods System
formulas.notes: An international monetary order established in 1944, which created the International Monetary Fund (IMF) and the World Bank to promote economic stability and development.
